activities_projects.title: Hydrological Cycle Explanation
activities_projects.objective: Explain how the hydrological cycle makes water a renewable resource
steps: Draw the hydrological cycle diagram
Describe the processes of evaporation, condensation, precipitation and runoff
Explain how these processes continuously renew water resources
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students explain why water is considered a renewable resource
activities_projects.evaluation: Concept clarity and correct explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study of Water Scarcity
activities_projects.objective: Analyze reasons for water scarcity in a region
steps: Choose a city or region facing water scarcity
Identify at least three causes of the problem
Suggest possible solutions to manage water resources
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical understanding of water scarcity issues

activities_projects.title: Multipurpose River Project Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of dams in water resource management
steps: Select one multipurpose river project such as Bhakra Nangal or Hirakud
Describe its location, river, and purpose
Explain how it supports irrigation, electricity generation, and flood control
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand functions of multipurpose river projects

activities_projects.title: Advantages vs Disadvantages of Dams
steps: Create a two-column table
List at least five advantages of dams
List at least five disadvantages affecting environment and society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students evaluate both positive and negative impacts of dams

activities_projects.title: Rainwater Harvesting System Design
activities_projects.objective: Design a simple rainwater harvesting system
steps: Draw a rooftop rainwater harvesting system
Label components such as roof, pipes, filter and storage tank
Explain how collected rainwater can be reused
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the structure and function of rainwater harvesting
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of diagram and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Traditional Water Harvesting Methods Research
steps: Research three traditional water harvesting systems in India
Explain how each system works
Identify the regions where they are practiced
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand traditional ecological knowledge of water conservation

activities_projects.title: Water Consumption Calculation
steps: Record daily water usage for activities like bathing, cooking and cleaning
Estimate the amount of water used for each activity
Calculate the total daily water consumption
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand patterns of water consumption

activities_projects.title: Industrial Water Pollution Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand how industries affect water resources
steps: Find examples of industries that pollute water resources
Describe the type of pollutants released
Suggest methods to reduce water pollution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand environmental impacts of industrial activities
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and relevance of examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Inter-State Water Disputes Analysis
steps: Research one inter-state water dispute in India
Explain the states involved and the river concerned
Describe the reasons for the conflict
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand political and geographical aspects of water disputes

activities_projects.title: Water Conservation Action Plan
activities_projects.objective: Develop strategies to conserve water locally
steps: List common ways water is wasted in homes or schools
Suggest at least five practical water conservation methods
Prepare a short action plan for implementing them
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop responsible attitudes toward water conservation
